section_name: Prohibition of Operation on Highway by Municipality, County, or Department
chapter_title: Operation of Bicycles & Mopeds, Golf Carts, and Other Low-powered Vehicles
text:
(a) A county or municipality may prohibit the operation of a golf cart on a highway under Section 551.403 (Operation Authorized in Certain Areas) if the governing body of the county or municipality determines that the prohibition is necessary in the interest of safety.

(b) The Texas Department of Transportation may prohibit the operation of a golf cart on a highway under Section 551.403 (Operation Authorized in Certain Areas) if the department determines that the prohibition is necessary in the interest of safety.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 551. Operation of Bicycles & Mopeds, Golf Carts, and Other Low-powered Vehicles
##### § 551.4031 — Prohibition of Operation on Highway by Municipality, County, or Department

(a) A county or municipality may prohibit the operation of a golf cart on a highway under Section 551.403 (Operation Authorized in Certain Areas) if the governing body of the county or municipality determines that the prohibition is necessary in the interest of safety.

(b) The Texas Department of Transportation may prohibit the operation of a golf cart on a highway under Section 551.403 (Operation Authorized in Certain Areas) if the department determines that the prohibition is necessary in the interest of safety.

*Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 652 (H.B.1281), Sec. 2, eff. June 15, 2021.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A county or municipality may prohibit the operation of a golf cart on a highway under Section 551.403 (Operation Authorized in Certain Areas) if the governing body of the county or municipality determines that the prohibition is necessary in the interest of safety.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The Texas Department of Transportation may prohibit the operation of a golf cart on a highway under Section 551.403 (Operation Authorized in Certain Areas) if the department determines that the prohibition is necessary in the interest of safety.

history.raw: Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 652 (H.B.1281), Sec. 2, eff. June 15, 2021.
